ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the city is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* The population of the city */,
  "elevation" REAL /* The elevation of the city in meters */,
  "urban_area_km2" REAL /* The urban area of the city in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "data_source_type" TEXT /* The type of data source */,
  "data_source_url" TEXT /* The URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_mapping_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* The institution or organization the user belongs to */,
  "department" TEXT /* The department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data entry was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_reason" TEXT /* The reason for accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data quality log */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being checked */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date when the data quality check was performed */,
  "check_description" TEXT /* Description of the data quality check */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken as a result of the check */,
  "data_quality_issue" TEXT /* A description of the data quality issue encountered */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* The resolution or action taken to address the data quality issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)
